summary:
You are given two integer arrays, preorder and inorder, which list the values of the same binary tree as visited by a preorder traversal and an inorder traversal respectively. Reconstruct the tree and return its root.

The first value of preorder identifies the root; locating that value in inorder splits the remaining values into the left and right subtrees, which you can build recursively.

examples.explanation: 3 is the root; in inorder, 9 falls to its left and 15,20,7 to its right, giving 9 as the left child and the subtree rooted at 20 on the right.
A single value produces a tree with just that root node.

constraints: All values are unique, and inorder is a permutation of preorder.
